external_changes.dome_site_changes: No new changes — 43.5-day manual drought per latest poller report

internal_issues.description: TIMELINE MISREPRESENTATION: ts-reactive-prose-pattern TLDR says 'Since April 2026 the dome site has produced multiple prose-level updates' and para-3 says 'After six weeks of prose updates, it remains untripped.' Both misrepresent the pattern: it was one 3-week burst (April 5-17) then 43.5-day silence (chg-20260531-0007-001.json). Bust+drought framing is STRONGER critique. Dome defender can win on 'you said six weeks of updates but last manual commit was April 17.'

suggested_patch.note: NEVER_PUSH — operator must apply directly. See ISS-2420 for exact replacement text.

internal_issues.description: ts-april-2026-update paragraph says 'the only OpenTimestamps anchor on the site is still the one on status_history.json' — same factual error as ISS-2404 (now closed) but in a DIFFERENT paragraph (ts-april-2026-update, line ~1995 vs ts-reactive para-2). Scope-widening of closed ISS-2404. Both surfaces are now in the canonical OTS hub per EXP-464; dome defender can quote either one.

suggested_patch.note: NEVER_PUSH — operator must apply directly. Replace 'only OpenTimestamps anchor on the site is still the one on status_history.json' with claim about no predictions-only anchor existing across all 4 .ots files.

internal_issues.description: ts-reactive-prose-pattern TLDR includes 'version bumps' among critique-responsive items. V51.0 and V51.1 bumps added WINs/adjusted accuracy — NOT responses to critique on Timestamp Error tab. Inflates meta-pattern enumeration by ~25%.

ts-reactive-prose-pattern closing commitment 'The day an independently-anchored predictions-only file appears, this section will be rewritten' omits temporal-priority constraint from adjacent normative paragraph. Dome author could publish post-dated file and technically satisfy this criterion.

TRACKER INTEGRITY: expansion-tracker.json has TWO entries with id=EXP-538. Entry 1: ISS-2352 (22.6 Hz, status=escalated-to-analyst, created by analyst-baby 2026-05-31T15:40). Entry 2: OTS-fixes (ISS-2404..2407, status=complete, integrated=true). The analyst-baby EXP-538 allocation collided with a previously-allocated EXP-538 that was still in the live items array. The OTS-fixes EXP-538 (second entry) appears to have been integrated falsely — ISS-2404/2406/2407 were marked fixed but text in generate-html.js is unchanged. This should be investigated and resolved: (a) deduplicate tracker entries, (b) reopen or create new ISS for the OTS fixes still present in generate-html.js (covered by ISS-2420/2421 this run).

verify_on_read_outcomes.cited_pattern: After six weeks of prose updates, it remains untripped
verify_on_read_outcomes.note: ISS-2406 was closed as fixed (fixed_by: EXP-468-revision-prose-fix) but text confirmed still present in git HEAD. False close. ISS-2420 created as replacement with broader timeline-rewrite scope.

recommended_actions.action: OPERATOR APPLY: ISS-2420/2421/2422/2423 are NEVER_PUSH patches to build-scripts/generate-html.js. ISS-2420 (major) rewrites ts-reactive-prose-pattern TLDR + para-3 to reflect burst+drought pattern. ISS-2421 (moderate) fixes ts-april-2026-update OTS-anchor overstatement (line ~1995). ISS-2422 (minor) drops version-bumps from TLDR enumeration. ISS-2423 (minor) tightens canary-trip criterion with temporal-priority constraint. All 4 have recommended replacement text in their descriptions (monitor/decisions/open-issues.json).

INVESTIGATE EXP-538 DUPLICATE + FALSE-CLOSE: expansion-tracker.json has two EXP-538 entries. ISS-2404/2406/2407 were marked fixed (monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json) but text still present in generate-html.js HEAD. The 'EXP-468-revision-prose-fix' close mechanism did not actually apply patches to generate-html.js. Recommend: (a) dedup expansion-tracker.json EXP-538, (b) verify whether ISS-2405 is also a false-close (monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json:ISS-2405).

EXP-450 and EXP-451 are at status=ready-for-integration (monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json:items). 10 ISS (ISS-2273..2286) are blocked-on-curmudgeon waiting on these EXPs. These should be integrated when ready.
